_note: Curated candidate registry for Florida 2026 (demo data). Candidate IDs are stable slugs (kebab-case) referenced by elections.json. Stances are realistic but editorialized for demonstration — replace with primary-sourced candidate statements in production.
_source: Manually curated. Candidate photos are placeholders; fundraising is illustrative.

fl-cand-byron-donalds.name: Byron Donalds
fl-cand-byron-donalds.bio: Byron Donalds has represented Florida's 19th congressional district since 2021 and previously served in the Florida House. He is running on a platform focused on economic growth, border security, and expanding school choice statewide.

top_issues.name: Economy
top_issues.stance: Pro-growth tax reform; reduce state regulatory burden on small business; keep Florida income-tax-free.

top_issues.name: Education
top_issues.stance: Expand universal school choice; strengthen civic curriculum; reform university governance.

top_issues.name: Public Safety
top_issues.stance: Continue anti-drug trafficking interdictions; fully staff sheriff's offices; support E-Verify enforcement.

top_issues.name: Housing
top_issues.stance: Remove local permitting barriers; expand Live Local Act incentives for workforce housing.

top_issues.name: Insurance
top_issues.stance: Continue property-insurance litigation reform; expand Citizens' depopulation pipeline.

fl-cand-paul-renner.name: Paul Renner
fl-cand-paul-renner.current_office: Former Speaker, Florida House of Representatives
fl-cand-paul-renner.bio: Paul Renner served as Speaker of the Florida House from 2022 to 2024, presiding over major reforms in education, litigation, and property insurance. He is running on a platform of experienced conservative governance.

top_issues.name: Education
top_issues.stance: Cement the universal school-choice expansion and protect civics reforms.

top_issues.name: Insurance
top_issues.stance: Continue tort-reform track; target fraud in auto-glass and roof claims.

top_issues.name: Public Safety
top_issues.stance: Invest in deputy training academies; maintain sentencing enhancements.

top_issues.name: Property Rights
top_issues.stance: Strengthen Burt Harris protections; cap local impact fees.

top_issues.name: Taxes
top_issues.stance: Permanent sales-tax holidays; no state income tax; cap local property-tax growth.

endorsements.name: Former FL Senate President Kathleen Passidomo

fl-cand-james-uthmeier.name: James Uthmeier
fl-cand-james-uthmeier.bio: James Uthmeier was appointed Attorney General in 2025. Previously he served as Governor DeSantis's chief of staff and general counsel. He is running for a full term on a platform of border enforcement, consumer protection, and transnational crime.

top_issues.name: Public Safety
top_issues.stance: Expand state task forces on human trafficking and fentanyl interdiction.

top_issues.name: Consumer Protection
top_issues.stance: Aggressive enforcement against insurance bad-faith and data-broker abuses.

top_issues.name: Immigration
top_issues.stance: Litigate to defend state-level immigration enforcement authority.

top_issues.name: Rule of Law
top_issues.stance: Challenge federal overreach; defend Florida statutes in court.

experience.role: Chief of Staff, Office of the Governor
General Counsel, Office of the Governor

fl-cand-jose-javier-rodriguez.name: José Javier Rodríguez
fl-cand-jose-javier-rodriguez.current_office: Former Assistant Secretary, U.S. Department of Labor
fl-cand-jose-javier-rodriguez.bio: José Javier Rodríguez is a former state senator and U.S. Department of Labor official. He is running for Attorney General on a platform of consumer protection, civil rights, and restoring the office's independence.

top_issues.name: Consumer Protection
top_issues.stance: Rebuild wage-theft unit; prosecute price-gouging aggressively after storms.

top_issues.name: Civil Rights
top_issues.stance: Re-establish civil rights division; vigorous voting-rights enforcement.

top_issues.name: Environment
top_issues.stance: Hold polluters accountable; pursue PFAS contamination cases.

top_issues.name: Housing
top_issues.stance: Enforce against predatory landlords and mortgage fraud.

experience.role: Assistant Secretary, U.S. Department of Labor

fl-cand-maxwell-frost.name: Maxwell Frost
fl-cand-maxwell-frost.bio: Maxwell Frost is the first Gen Z member of Congress, representing Florida's 10th district since 2023. He is seeking re-election on a platform of housing affordability, gun safety, and Medicare expansion.

top_issues.name: Housing
top_issues.stance: Federal rental-assistance expansion; ban corporate rent-fixing algorithms.

top_issues.name: Gun Safety
top_issues.stance: Universal background checks; assault-weapons ban; youth-focused prevention.

top_issues.name: Healthcare
top_issues.stance: Lower Medicare age; cap insulin and inhaler prices.

top_issues.name: Voting Rights
top_issues.stance: Pass the Freedom to Vote Act.

experience.role: National Organizing Director, March For Our Lives

fl-cand-willie-montague.name: Willie Montague
fl-cand-willie-montague.bio: Willie Montague is a pastor and nonprofit founder who ran for FL-10 in 2020 and 2022. He is running on a platform of economic opportunity in urban communities, school choice, and family values.

top_issues.name: Education
top_issues.stance: Expand federal school-choice dollars; support HBCUs and trade programs.

top_issues.name: Economy
top_issues.stance: Opportunity zone extensions; small-business tax credits in underserved neighborhoods.

top_issues.name: Public Safety
top_issues.stance: Restore broken-windows policing in partnership with community leaders.

top_issues.name: Family
top_issues.stance: Federal paid-family-leave via tax credit; fatherhood initiative funding.

fl-cand-anna-eskamani.name: Anna V. Eskamani
fl-cand-anna-eskamani.bio: Anna Eskamani has represented FL House District 42 since 2018. She is seeking re-election on a platform of reproductive rights, renters' protections, and transparency reforms.

top_issues.name: Reproductive Rights
top_issues.stance: Defend Amendment 4 implementation; repeal parental-consent override.

top_issues.name: Housing
top_issues.stance: Statewide rent-stabilization framework; just-cause eviction protections.

top_issues.name: Labor
top_issues.stance: Repeal preemption of local minimum-wage laws; restore collective-bargaining protections.

experience.role: Planned Parenthood of Southwest and Central Florida (exec)

fl-cand-bonnie-jackson.name: Bonnie Jackson
fl-cand-bonnie-jackson.bio: Bonnie Jackson is a small-business owner making her first run for office, focused on tax relief, parental rights, and public safety.

top_issues.name: Taxes
top_issues.stance: Cap local millage growth; permanent back-to-school sales tax holiday.

top_issues.name: Parental Rights
top_issues.stance: Expand parental curriculum notification; defend library-content review laws.

top_issues.name: Public Safety
top_issues.stance: Fund school resource officers in every campus; support sheriff's offices.

fl-cand-ashley-moody-sen.name: Ashley Moody
fl-cand-ashley-moody-sen.bio: Ashley Moody is a Republican candidate for the U.S. Senate from Florida, currently serving as the incumbent.

top_issues.name: Law Enforcement & Public Safety
top_issues.stance: A former Florida Attorney General (2019–2025), Moody emphasizes support for law enforcement and public safety. Has sponsored law-enforcement legislation in the Senate, including a bill to increase penalties for harming animals used in law enforcement; crime and law enforcement is the most frequent policy area of her sponsored bills.

top_issues.name: Education & School Choice
top_issues.stance: Sponsored education legislation in the Senate, including the Home School Graduation Recognition Act, the FLEX Act, and the No Aid for Ghost Students Act.

top_issues.name: Healthcare & Program Integrity
top_issues.stance: Sponsored health legislation focused on program integrity, including the STOP FRAUD in Medicaid Act.

top_issues.name: Immigration & Border Security
top_issues.stance: States securing the border as a priority and has sponsored immigration-related legislation; as Florida Attorney General she joined legal challenges to federal immigration policies.

fl-cand-alexander-vindman-sen.name: Alexander Vindman
fl-cand-alexander-vindman-sen.bio: Alexander Vindman is a Democratic candidate for the U.S. Senate from Florida. A retired U.S. Army lieutenant colonel, he served as Director for European Affairs on the National Security Council and testified during the 2019 House impeachment inquiry. He is an author and national-security commentator.

experience.role: Director for European Affairs, U.S. National Security Council

fl-cand-alexander-vindman-sen.data_source: OpenFEC + curated (Wikipedia, CBS Miami, Florida Politics)

top_issues.stance: Sponsored legislation to repeal the Hughes Amendment to the Firearm Owners' Protection Act.

top_issues.name: Economic & Tax Policy
top_issues.stance: Sponsored tax and savings legislation, including the Save for Success Act.
